summary: This 30-day post-incident review covered the March 10-18 Detect module outage, which caused six hours of complete threat monitoring blindness for 47 affected accounts due to a single point of failure in the event ingestion pipeline. The engineering team confirmed fixes including redundant processing nodes and a circuit breaker pattern, both deployed by March 29th, with a clean 30-day window since. Customer Success raised concerns about two key accounts — Meridian Financial (August renewal at risk due to compliance audit impact) and Treswick Healthcare (HIPAA documentation concerns) — and the team discussed potential remediation strategies including early access to the Detect telemetry dashboard and a Comply v2 upsell conversation. The team aligned on several follow-up actions including drafting a customer-facing incident summary and improving internal communications protocols.
actionItems: Chris Lee: Talk to Priya about getting Meridian Financial into an early access or beta program for the expanded event telemetry dashboard by mid-May
Tyler Washington: Draft a customer-facing incident summary document and share with Diana for tone review by end of week
Diana Reeves: Check Treswick Healthcare's contract to determine if they are on Comply and explore a platform expansion conversation
Chris Lee: Update on-call runbook to ensure Customer Success receives a heads-up notification whenever a circuit breaker activation occurs

keyMoments.text: Diana reveals that Meridian Financial faced a real compliance audit problem due to the six-hour monitoring gap, with renewal at risk in August
keyMoments.speaker: Diana Reeves

keyMoments.text: Team identifies opportunity to offer Meridian Financial early access to the expanded event telemetry dashboard as a service recovery gesture
keyMoments.speaker: Diana Reeves

keyMoments.text: Chris Lee connects Comply v2's new on-demand HIPAA reporting as a potential solution for Treswick Healthcare's regulatory documentation concerns
keyMoments.speaker: Chris Lee

keyMoments.text: Diana requests that engineering notify Customer Success via Slack whenever a circuit breaker activation occurs, even if it is a non-event for customers
keyMoments.speaker: Diana Reeves
